full_prompt: Design a midship structural bay of a 10.0 m aluminum crew workboat. The workboat is a 10.0 m LOA x 3.2 m beam x 1.2 m depth (molded) x 0.70 m design draft aluminum planing hull carrying a maximum payload of 1.5 t plus four crew, powered by twin 150 kW outboards. The midship bay spans 2.0 m along the length between transverse frames and includes bottom shell, side shell, deck plating, one longitudinal centerline girder, and two bottom longitudinals, built from marine-grade aluminum alloy 5083-H116 (yield 215 MPa, ultimate 305 MPa, E 70 GPa, density 2660 kg/m^3). Plating scantlings must satisfy the ABS High-Speed Naval Craft or ABS HSNC / ABS Aluminum Pleasure Craft equivalent; apply the ABS HSC slamming pressure formula with a design vertical acceleration of 1.5 g at LCG yielding a bottom slamming pressure of 68 kPa, a side shell pressure of 22 kPa, and a deck design pressure of 6 kPa. The midship bay must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) maximum von Mises stress in any plating or stiffener under the combined slamming (bottom) + hydrostatic (side) + deck (top) load case must not exceed 143 MPa which is yield divided by 1.5, (R2) maximum plating deflection under bottom slamming pressure must not exceed panel_span/100 which is 6 mm for the 600 mm longitudinal spacing, (R3) a linear buckling eigenvalue of the side shell panel under a longitudinal hog bending stress of 60 MPa must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0, (R4) intact stability GMt must be at least 0.30 m at design displacement per ISO 12217-1 category B, and (R5) the midship section modulus about the neutral axis must equal or exceed the ABS HSNC minimum for this length-to-beam ratio at 3.1e-4 m^3. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required; wave bending and slamming pressures are prescribed. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly of the midship bay with plating and stiffeners as sub-bodies plus a scantling report listing maximum von Mises, plating deflection, first buckling load factor, GMt, and section modulus.
short_prompt:
Design the midship structural bay (2.0 m long between transverse frames) of a 10 m LOA x 3.2 m beam x 1.2 m depth aluminum planing workboat, design draft 0.7 m, payload 1.5 t + 4 crew, twin 150 kW outboards. The bay is welded marine aluminum (5083-H116 class) and includes bottom shell, side shell, deck plating, a centerline girder, and two bottom longitudinals.

Scantlings are per ABS HSNC / ABS Aluminum Pleasure Craft with a design vertical acceleration of 1.5 g at LCG. Transverse frames act as simple supports at the ends of the bay; centerline is a symmetry plane.

Load cases.
- LC1 bottom slam pressure: 68 kPa on the bottom shell.
- LC2 side hydrostatic pressure: 22 kPa on the side shell.
- LC3 deck design pressure: 6 kPa on the weather deck.
- LC4 longitudinal hog bending: 60 MPa stress on the side shell.

Also check intact GMt per ISO 12217-1 and the ABS HSNC midship section modulus floor.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: GMt and righting arm curve are closed-form from hydrostatics of the hull form, not FEA.

prompt.title: 10 m aluminum workboat midship structural bay
prompt.brief: Design a 2.0 m midship bay of a 10.0 m LOA x 3.2 m beam aluminum workboat in 5083-H116 with ABS HSNC slamming 68 kPa, side 22 kPa, deck 6 kPa, meeting stress, deflection, buckling, stability, and section-modulus floors.

boundary_conditions.frame_edges: transverse frames at both ends of the bay act as simple supports

objective.primary: pass all scantling and stability checks

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ABS HSNC minimum for L/B ratio

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for plating, beam for stiffeners

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Slamming is scope-reduced to quasi-static pressure per ABS.

eval_coverage: hydrostatic stability GMt coupling with FEA structural pass/fail
buckling check under longitudinal bending stress
